description: RFC-0014 P2 new-name (OTel/semconv) copy of the microservices dashboard, soaking side-by-side with the legacy-named original for old-vs-new comparison during the dual-emit period. Queries use OTel HTTP semconv metrics (http_server_request_duration_seconds, http_server_{request,response}_body_size_bytes), OTel Go runtime metrics (go_goroutine_count, go_memory_*), and gRPC RED (rpc_server_call_duration_seconds). The legacy dashboard retires at P3.

panels.title: 99th Percentile Response Success
panels.description: 99% of requests complete faster than this. Detects worst-case latency and outliers.

panels.title: 95th Percentile Response Success
panels.description: 95% of requests complete faster than this. Key metric for user experience.

panels.title: 50th Percentile Response Success
panels.description: Median response time. Represents typical user experience.

panels.title: Total RPS (All Requests)
panels.description: Total requests per second including all HTTP status codes (2xx, 4xx, 5xx). Use this to monitor overall traffic volume.

panels.title: Success RPS (2xx)
panels.description: Successful requests per second (HTTP 2xx responses). This represents productive traffic.

panels.title: Error RPS (4xx/5xx)
panels.description: Failed requests per second (HTTP 4xx/5xx responses). Monitor this to detect issues quickly.

panels.title: Success Rate %
panels.description: Percentage of successful requests (2xx / total).

panels.title: Error Rate %
panels.description: Percentage of failed requests (4xx/5xx / total).

panels.title: Apdex Score
panels.description: User satisfaction score (0-1). Satisfying: <0.5s, Tolerating: 0.5-2s, Frustrated: >2s. Formula: (satisfying + 0.5 * tolerating) / total requests. Handles zero traffic gracefully.

panels.title: Total Request
panels.description: Total requests in selected time range. Correlate with traffic spikes or incidents.

panels.title: Status Code Distribution
panels.description: HTTP status code distribution by rate (req/sec). Shows real-time traffic breakdown by response code. Expected: ~95% codes 2xx.

panels.title: Total Requests by Endpoint
panels.description: Request distribution across endpoints. Identifies hot paths and traffic patterns.

panels.title: Request Rate by Endpoint
panels.description: Request rate per endpoint over time. Detects traffic spikes per API.

panels.title: RPS by Endpoint
panels.description: Request rate per endpoint path. Shows traffic distribution across different API endpoints over time.

panels.title: Request Rate by Method and Endpoint
panels.description: Breakdown of request rate by HTTP method (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE) and endpoint path. Helps identify traffic patterns and detect unusual method-specific spikes.

targets.expr: (sum(rate(http_server_request_duration_seconds_count{service_name=~"$app",  http_response_status_code=~"4..|5..", http_route!=""}[$rate])) by (http_request_method, http_route) / sum(rate(http_server_request_duration_seconds_count{service_name=~"$app", http_route!=""}[$rate])) by (http_request_method, http_route)) * 100 > 0

panels.title: Error Rate by Method and Endpoint
panels.description: Percentage of 4xx/5xx errors by HTTP method and endpoint. Only displays combinations with error rate > 0%. Use this to quickly identify problematic endpoints.

panels.title: Client Errors (4xx)
panels.description: Client-side errors (400-499) in req/sec by service. High 4xx rates indicate API misuse, invalid requests, authentication issues, or bad client behavior. Common codes: 400 (Bad Request), 401 (Unauthorized), 403 (Forbidden), 404 (Not Found), 429 (Rate Limited).

panels.title: Server Errors (5xx)
panels.description: Server-side errors (500-599) in req/sec by service. High 5xx rates indicate service degradation, bugs, infrastructure issues, or dependency failures. Common codes: 500 (Internal Server Error), 502 (Bad Gateway), 503 (Service Unavailable), 504 (Gateway Timeout). Requires immediate investigation.

panels.title: Response time 95th percentile
panels.description: p95 response time per endpoint. Identifies slow APIs.

panels.title: Response time 50th percentile
panels.description: Median response time per endpoint. Shows typical performance baseline.

panels.title: Response time 99th percentile
panels.description: p99 response time per endpoint. Highlights tail latency issues.

panels.title: Memory In-Use
panels.description: OTel Go runtime: bytes currently in use (go.memory.used).

panels.title: Memory Allocation Rate
panels.description: Heap allocation rate (objects/s) from the OTel Go runtime. The OTel runtime emits no GC-pause metric; rising allocation rate is the leading indicator of GC pressure.

panels.title: Goroutines
panels.description: Goroutine and OS thread count. Steadily increasing goroutines indicates goroutine leak (forgotten defer, unclosed channels). Stable count is normal.

targets.expr: sum by (service_name) (go_memory_used_bytes{ service_name=~"$app"}) / sum by (service_name) (go_memory_gc_goal_bytes{ service_name=~"$app"})

panels.title: GC Pacing Pressure (used / goal)
panels.description: Heap used vs the GC pacer goal from the OTel Go runtime. Sustained values near 1.0 mean the heap keeps hitting the GC goal — memory pressure / frequent GC cycles.

panels.title: Total Network Traffic per Service
panels.description: Total HTTP traffic (TX/RX) by ALL pods in the service. Use for bandwidth planning and cost estimation. Note: Only HTTP body size, not TCP/IP overhead.

panels.title: gRPC Server RPS by Method
panels.description: Server-side gRPC request rate per method (rpc_method, e.g. auth.v1.AuthService/GetMe). Source: rpc_server_call_duration_seconds_count.

panels.title: gRPC Client RPS by Method
panels.description: Client-side gRPC request rate per method and upstream (server_address). Source: rpc_client_call_duration_seconds_count.

panels.title: gRPC Server Error Rate (non-OK)
panels.description: Server-side gRPC error rate per method, counting calls where rpc_response_status_code != OK. Source: rpc_server_call_duration_seconds_count.

panels.title: gRPC Client Error Rate (non-OK)
panels.description: Client-side gRPC error rate per method and upstream, counting calls where rpc_response_status_code != OK. Source: rpc_client_call_duration_seconds_count.

panels.title: gRPC Server P95 Latency
panels.description: Server-side gRPC 95th percentile call duration per method. Source: rpc_server_call_duration_seconds_bucket.

panels.title: gRPC Client P95 Latency
panels.description: Client-side gRPC 95th percentile call duration per method and upstream. Source: rpc_client_call_duration_seconds_bucket.

panels.title: gRPC Server RPS per Callee
panels.description: Server-side gRPC request rate aggregated per callee service (app). Source: rpc_server_call_duration_seconds_count.

targets.expr: sum by (service_name) (rate(rpc_server_call_duration_seconds_count{ service_name=~"$app", rpc_response_status_code!="OK"}[$rate])) / sum by (service_name) (rate(rpc_server_call_duration_seconds_count{ service_name=~"$app"}[$rate]))

panels.title: gRPC Server Error Ratio per Callee
panels.description: Share of server-side gRPC calls with rpc_response_status_code != OK, per callee service (app). Source: rpc_server_call_duration_seconds_count.

panels.title: gRPC Server P95 Latency per Callee
panels.description: Server-side gRPC 95th percentile call duration per callee service (app). Source: rpc_server_call_duration_seconds_bucket.

panels.title: DB query p95 by service
panels.description: otelpgx per-statement latency (DB-scale buckets, pkg v0.24.0). Query ops only.

panels.title: DB p95 by operation type
panels.description: query / batch / prepare / connect / acquire — split by op.

panels.title: DB operation errors
panels.description: otelpgx non-ErrNoRows operation errors. Healthy = no series/0.

panels.title: Pool in-flight (acquired conns)
panels.description: Connections currently checked out ≈ concurrent DB work in flight.

panels.title: Pool saturation (acquired / max)
panels.description: Sustained ≥80% = acquires start queueing (PgxPoolNearExhaustion).

panels.title: Pool contention (waiting acquires)
panels.description: Acquires that had to wait for a free conn — earliest saturation signal.

title: Microservices (OTel) — local
